what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG volume icon showing a speaker shape with two sound-wave arcs, using stroked paths and currentColor.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with no explicit width or height.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling sets width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The first path draws the speaker body and cone.
The second path draws two sound-wave arcs.
standouts: Speaker body and sound waves are encoded directly as path data.
conventions: Follows common line-icon conventions: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, rounded stroke endings, currentColor inheritance.
Keeps all geometry inline within a single SVG element.
Uses path commands rather than shape primitives for the icon geometry.
concerns: No title, description, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility context.
notes: A small themeable SVG asset for representing audible volume or speaker output.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming UI rather than inside this SVG?
Is the missing explicit width and height intentional for CSS-controlled sizing?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename's implied purpose matches the content: it renders a volume/speaker icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable volume control icon that inherits text color and scales via the SVG viewBox.
contribution: Provides a compact currentColor speaker-with-sound-waves SVG asset suitable for audio, volume, or sound-state controls in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 stroke-only SVG volume icon with a speaker body and two sound-wave arcs using currentColor styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for supplying a reusable, themeable volume/speaker icon asset.
